PostgreSQL 16 supports local, host, hostssl, hostnossl, hostgssenc, and hostnogssenc record types. local is for Unix-domain sockets. host matches TCP/IP whether or not SSL or GSSAPI encryption is used. hostssl matches only SSL connections and requires both PostgreSQL to be built with SSL support and the ssl setting to be enabled. hostgssenc matches only GSSAPI-encrypted TCP/IP connections and requires GSSAPI support.

The address field in PostgreSQL 16 can use CIDR-style IP ranges, explicit IP address plus mask columns, host names, or the keywords all, samehost, and samenet. samehost matches the server's own IP addresses, and samenet matches addresses in directly connected subnets.

PostgreSQL 16 lists methods including trust, reject, scram-sha-256, md5, password, gss, sspi, ident, peer, ldap, radius, cert, pam, and bsd. trust allows the connection without checking credentials, while reject blocks it unconditionally. ident is only for TCP/IP and uses the client ident service; if ident is specified for a local connection, PostgreSQL uses peer instead. peer is available only for local connections. hostssl rules can also use the clientcert option to require a trusted client certificate.
